tweets.author: benitoz
tweets.text:
Hock Tan: $NVDA still the platform king — but $AVGO just put $100B+ AI chip revenue for ’27 on the table with 6 XPU customers incl OpenAI. 

Networking up 60%. Supply chain locked through ’28.

The bears want you to see AVGO vs NVDA. I see both confirming the same thing — 2027 https://t.co/jQTz7lCiGw

tweets.author: benitoz
tweets.text:
Even Hock Tan admits it his custom ASIC customers HAVE to compete with NVIDIA.

Every generation, Jensen raises the bar

Everyone else is chasing

Broadcom’s job? Help hyperscalers try to keep up

That’s the strongest $NVDA bull case you’ll hear on a competitor’s earnings call https://t.co/wdxUNORkSX

tweets.author: benitoz
tweets.text:
Custom ASICs use copper. 

NVIDIA training clusters use optics. 

Hock’s copper comments are about inference  not 100K+ GPU fabrics where copper can’t reach. 

Different workloads. Different physics. Don’t confuse the two anon

$lite $crdo $cohr $alab

tweets.author: benitoz
tweets.text: @firstadopter Key nuance here — this is an inference play. XPU + Ethernet + copper scales perfectly for inference workloads. Training still needs NVDA's full stack: NVLink, InfiniBand, optical. Two different hardware paths emerging. Bullish both.

tweets.author: benitoz
tweets.text: @draecomino Wow. Prescient and presidential

tweets.author: benitoz
tweets.text: @zephyr_z9 I use the free version to test hotel WiFi speeds lol

tweets.author: benitoz
tweets.text:
GPT-5.4 benchmarks just dropped

Don’t look at the scores, look at the fine print:
“run with maximum available reasoning effort”

Better models don’t reduce compute. They increase it.

More tokens, more KV cache, more memory bandwidth.

The Memory Wars are just getting started. https://t.co/eIfmUbYCQ8

tweets.author: benitoz
tweets.text: My good friend @success_cw made a video based on my Groq Nvda System GTC thesis will look like take a look and give him a follow  https://t.co/SXR4qRbApN https://t.co/q3j9MT6kXT

tweets.author: benitoz
tweets.text: @JulienTechInvst @firstadopter Great point you're right. Optics are needed the moment you leave the rack regardless of workload. BER doesn't care if it's training or inference. The copper debate is really about scale-up domain size, not whether optics are needed. They are. Appreciate the sharpening.

High crude px -> more EVs
High NG px -> more coal + renewables

For latter, they took 10:29 to 11:53 to charge
